steps.title: Separate two full clauses
steps.content: The words before the blank form a complete clause about the sculptures challenging viewers. The words after the blank form another complete clause: the works in the exhibition were no different.

steps.title: Choose a valid boundary
steps.content: Choice B incorrectly sends the second clause after to, Choice C creates a comma splice, and Choice D creates a run-on. A semicolon correctly joins the two independent clauses. The answer is A.
